research_brief.summary: A weeks-long dancing outbreak in Strasbourg remains historically documented but medically unresolved.

claims.text: The outbreak began in Strasbourg in July 1518.
Accounts identify a woman commonly called Frau Troffea among the first people to dance.
Civic authorities at one stage arranged musicians and spaces for the dancers.
The episode lasted for weeks and involved many residents.

disputed_points: The cause and exact number of participants or deaths remain disputed.

sources.title: Dancing plague of 1518

song_package.historical_topic: The Dancing Plague of 1518 in Strasbourg
song_package.factual_summary: In July 1518, a dancing outbreak spread through Strasbourg; authorities tried music and designated spaces, but its cause remains disputed.
song_package.lyrics:
Strasbourg woke to dancing feet,
July burned beneath the street.
Frau Troffea turned alone,
then the fever found the town.

Dance, dance, nobody knows,
why the city could not close.
Dance, dance, drums in the square,
hundreds moving through the air.

Councillors called the pipers in,
built a stage to cure the spin.
Weeks went by; the records blur,
saints and sickness, heat and fear.

Dance, dance, nobody knows,
where the final footstep goes.
Five long centuries ask us why—
what made Strasbourg dance, not die?
song_package.suno_style_prompt: Dark medieval folk cabaret, 96 BPM, minor mode, hurdy-gurdy, frame drum and bowed strings, immediate clear vocal entrance, lean theatrical arrangement, crisp consonants, recurring chant hook, haunting unresolved final chord.
song_package.vocal_direction: Enter on beat one; articulate Strasbourg and Troffea clearly; verses narrate, hook becomes a restrained communal chant.

song_package.suggested_youtube_title: Why Strasbourg Couldn’t Stop Dancing in 1518
song_package.suggested_description: In July 1518, Strasbourg residents began dancing for weeks. City leaders even brought in musicians—but the cause remains disputed. This Archive Song follows only source-mapped claims and keeps uncertain details labeled.
